title: West Bank Attacks Surge During Eid — Israeli Settler Violence
detail: Palestinian weekly reports documented a surge in Israeli settler violence in the West Bank during the Eid period in late March 2026. On March 21, heightened clashes occurred following an incident in which one Israeli settler was killed. The West Bank security situation remains severely strained, linked to ongoing IDF operations and settler expansion. Human rights organizations documented multiple incidents of settler attacks on Palestinian villages, with IDF forces accused of standing by or actively supporting settler operations. The UN has repeatedly warned that West Bank instability could undermine the fragile Gaza ceasefire.

title: Hamas Quietly Rebuilding Infrastructure in Gaza Amid Ceasefire
detail: Analysis published March 21, 2026 by the Algemeiner and backed by intelligence reporting indicates that Hamas has been quietly rebuilding its infrastructure in Gaza during the Phase One ceasefire period. Analysts note Hamas tightening its civilian administrative grip in northern Gaza, reestablishing checkpoints, and reorganizing its governance structures. IDF intelligence estimates Hamas retains significant capacity to reconstitute militarily. Israel cites this rebuild as justification for continued targeted strikes and resistance to any withdrawal before full disarmament.
